title: LEX - Lead Exchange Standard - Message Envelope
description: Root schema for all LEX messages (LEAD, ASSET, ACKNOWLEDGMENT, SUBSCRIPTION, LEAD_CLOSURE)

dataGovernance.description: Optional organizational data governance declaration. Controls forwarding policy, analytics use, and retention. See specs/LEX_DATA_GOVERNANCE.md.

MessageHeader.description: Standard header for all LEX messages

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when message was created

senderId.description: Identifier of system sending this message

receiverId.description: Identifier of system receiving this message (or BROADCAST)

encryptionMethod.description: Encryption method used for message transmission

correlationId.description: Optional ID to correlate related messages (response ↔ request)

replyTo.description: Optional identifier to send response to (if different from senderId)

signature.description: Base64-encoded message signature (see LexSecurity). Spec: LEX_SPECIFICATION §5

ExtensionsArray.description: Typed, namespaced, versioned custom data envelopes. See specs/LEX_EXTENSION_STANDARD.md. Passthrough Obligation: unknown entries MUST be forwarded intact.

namespace.description: Dot-separated namespace. Format: {org}.{division}.{purpose}. lex.core.* is reserved for the LEX working group.

version.description: Semantic version of the extension schema (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)

producer.description: LEX organization ID of the system that produced this extension entry

producedAt.description: Timestamp when this extension entry was produced

schemaUri.description: Optional URI to a JSON Schema for the data object. Resolving it is optional. Message is valid if absent or unreachable.

data.description: Extension payload — any valid JSON value (object, array, number, string, boolean)

DataGovernance.description: Organizational data governance declaration. See specs/LEX_DATA_GOVERNANCE.md. DG-RULE-1: dataOwner is the organization with direct contractual relationship to the data subject. DG-RULE-2: dataCustodian != dataOwner (DMS is a custodian, not an owner). DG-RULE-3: analyticsAllowed/trainingDataAllowed are explicit opt-in/opt-out signals. DG-RULE-4: Zero Trust forwarding — RESTRICTED policy must be checked before routing.

dataOwner.description: LEX org ID of the organization that owns this data. Usually the originating dealer or platform.

dataCustodian.description: Optional. LEX org ID of the system currently holding the data (e.g. DMS). Different from dataOwner when the holder is not the owner.

forwardingPolicy.description: OPEN: any LEX participant may forward. RESTRICTED: only allowedRecipients may receive. LOCKED: no forwarding beyond direct receiver.

allowedRecipients.description: Required when forwardingPolicy is RESTRICTED. LEX org IDs that may receive this message.

blockedRecipients.description: Explicit deny list. Applies even when forwardingPolicy is OPEN.

analyticsAllowed.description: Whether this data may be used in aggregated analytics products. false = opt-out.

trainingDataAllowed.description: Whether this data may be used to train ML or AI models. false = opt-out.

maxRetentionDays.description: Maximum days the receiving system may retain this data after declaredAt.

deleteOnClosure.description: If true, data must be deleted when the related lead is closed.

archiveOnClosure.description: If true, data must be archived (not deleted) when the related lead is closed.

auditRequired.description: When true, systems that forward this message SHOULD log the forwarding event.

governanceVersion.description: Version of this governance declaration (for audit trail purposes).

declaredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this governance declaration was made.

declaredBy.description: LEX org ID of the system/organization that declared this governance record. SHOULD match dataOwner.

aiGovernance.description: AI-specific governance sub-block. Present when message contains leadIntelligence or aiInsights. Spec: LEX_DATA_GOVERNANCE §6

aiGeneratedContentPresent.description: true if any field in this message was produced by AI or ML

humanReviewRequired.description: true if operators MUST apply human review before acting on AI content

explainabilityAvailable.description: true if the model can provide an explainability report on request

aiDecisionScope.description: Permitted scope of AI output usage by downstream systems

purposeLimitation.description: Human-readable description of permitted AI processing purpose

retentionLimitDays.description: Maximum days AI-generated artefacts may be retained by downstream systems

certificateId.description: Reference to certificate used for signing

ISO639Language.description: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., en-US, hi-IN, de-DE)

Currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., USD, INR, EUR)

LeadStatus.description: Current status of lead in lifecycle - supports non-linear progression

LeadType.description: Type of lead - helps track origination context and relationships

LeadSource.description: Where the lead originated. All values match LeadSource enum in MessageHeader.hx

ProductType.description: Canonical product type string — must be registered in ProductRegistry. UPPER_SNAKE_CASE, 2–60 chars. Examples: VEHICLE_NEW, VEHICLE_USED, MOTORCYCLE, SCOOTER, AIRCRAFT_NEW, LOCOMOTIVE, PASSENGER_TRAIN, TRAM_LRV, CARGO_VESSEL, SMARTPHONE, WIND_TURBINE, MEDICAL_DEVICE, MILITARY_VEHICLE, FLEET_ORDER. Full list: haxe/src/lex/registry/ProductRegistry.hx#seedStandardTypes

AssetClass.description: Broad asset class. All values match AssetClass enum in ProductRegistry.hx

MessageTTL.description: Time-to-live for message delivery. Spec: LEX_TRANSPORT_RESILIENCE §2

maxAgeSeconds.description: Delivery window in seconds. Defaults: LEAD=3600, ACK=300, ASSET/SUB=86400

onExpiry.description: Action when TTL expires: DLQ (dead-letter queue), DISCARD (drop), RETURN (return to sender)

RetryContext.description: Added by transport layer on re-delivery. Absent means first delivery. Spec: LEX_TRANSPORT_RESILIENCE §3

maxAttempts.description: Maximum attempts before routing to DLQ

managementSystemData.description: Management system (ERP/DMS) provider-specific identifiers and data

ownershipChain.description: Complete audit trail of message ownership
